# MFC in-app journey

: Saksham Srivastava
Created time: December 5, 2024 7:44 PM
Status: In progress
Last edited: January 5, 2025 4:07 PM

# **What problem are we solving?**

Currently for Volt in-app journeys customers are required to fetch from CAMS and KFin separately. This results in increased customer drop-off because of the following reasons:

- User comprehension: The initial limit fetched from CAMS is roughly 60% of the total limit of the customer, customers don’t clearly understand that they need to fetch from KFin to get their complete eligible limit.

The same is reflected in the funnel numbers below: 

- High friction: Two OTPs required for complete fetch.

[https://app.amplitude.com/analytics/volt-hq/chart/new/za7my7iy](https://app.amplitude.com/analytics/volt-hq/chart/new/za7my7iy)

---

Both the current problems can be solved by integrating MFC fetch into the app. 

# **How do we measure success?**

- Reduced customer drop-off on the fetch step by >20%

---

# **How are others solving this problem?**

---

# **What is the solution?**

## Requirements overview (optional)

Phase 1 requirements: 

- Integrate MFC fetch (CAS summary) on the fetch step.
- Handle error code for MFC fetch as per this [sheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1xSzlCnpbb0DFMN2AXEf9kqSW5Cf4IXTbboUoSISXf2w/edit?usp=sharing).
- Enable customer to select CAMS/KFin fetch flow if they want to from the current check eligibility page.
- Instrument all FE and BE events to new the MFC and older CAMS/KFin fetch flow to ensure proper tracking. Event requirements are here.
- Instrument FE and BE events on the pledge step to understand pledge errors customers are encountering at the pledge step.
- Run lender assignment BRE before showing customer the eligible credit limit, removing the need for customer to click on “Continue to check credit limit” button to get final limit.
- Create a new portfolio success page with the following CTA:
    - Set credit limit - This will move user to the set credit limit page
    - Refresh portfolio - This will move users to the “Check eligibility page” where if the customer wants to select the CAMS/KFin flow they can do so.
- I should be able to whitelist platforms that will have MFC flow enabled

Not included in Phase 1:

- Portfolio refreshes that happen after customers encountering pledge errors should not be moved to MFC for now

Risk callout:

- MFC CAS fetch doesn’t have validations such as Fatca
